ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for caramelised ginger chicken. FOODON_00002427 (chicken dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for caramelised ginger chicken preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for this specific prepared chicken dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of caramelised ginger chicken is approximately 250 g, typically comprising stir-fried chicken pieces with ginger, garlic, and caramelised onion sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, stir-fried with sauce, Chinese-style — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, stir-fried (NDB 05062) adapted with caramelisation sugar and soy sauce additions
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry for caramelised ginger chicken was found. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo stir-fried chicken dishes and USDA SR Legacy cooked stir-fried chicken (NDB 05062), with adjustments for caramelisation sugar (~5 g added sugar per 100 g), soy sauce sodium contribution (~180 mg/100 g), and cooking oil. Protein and fat values anchored to USDA SR Legacy stir-fried chicken breast/thigh composite. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate Chinese stir-fried chicken dish analogue; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K et al.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for caramelised ginger chicken. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous Chinese stir-fried chicken dishes with sauce from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The caramelisation sugar and soy sauce contribute modest carbohydrate (~9.8 g/100 g), but the high protein and fat content substantially blunts glycaemic response. GL per 250 g serving = (45 × 9.8 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100 g; ~1200 mg per serving) from soy sauce and caramelised sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100–150 g and request reduced-soy-sauce preparation, as a standard 250 g serving provides approximately 1200 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate added sugar from caramelisation (~5.2 g sugar/100 g) with low-to-moderate GI of 45
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (45) and high protein content make this dish relatively suitable for blood glucose management, but the added caramelisation sugar (~13 g per 250 g serving) should be factored into carbohydrate counting; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one serving and pair with non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (2.4 g/100 g) and high sodium (480 mg/100 g) with favourable lean protein content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish provides lean chicken protein beneficial for cardiovascular health, but the sodium content from soy sauce and the saturated fat from cooking oil warrant moderation; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit to one 150 g portion and choose preparations with reduced oil and sodium-reduced soy sauce.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Caramelised Ginger Chicken is a Chinese-style stir-fried chicken dish popular in Singapore, characterised by tender chicken pieces cooked with fresh ginger, garlic, and caramelised onion in a sweet-savoury sauce made from soy sauce and caramelised sugar. The dish is associated with Loong Dim Sum restaurant on North Canal Road, Singapore, where it is served as a main course at approximately SGD 21. The preparation involves stir-frying marinated chicken at high heat to achieve a crispy exterior, then finishing with a caramelised sauce that imparts a glossy, amber coating with a balance of sweetness, umami, and ginger heat. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 195 kcal per 100 g, with 18.5 g protein, 9.5 g fat, and 9.8 g carbohydrate. The protein content supports satiety and muscle maintenance. The sodium content is elevated (~480 mg/100 g) due to soy sauce, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycaemic index is estimated at 45 (low), as the high protein and fat content blunts postprandial glucose response despite the caramelisation sugar. The dish is halal-suitable and dairy-free. It is relevant for clinical dietary counselling in the context of sodium restriction, carbohydrate management in Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular risk reduction. Common serving context is as a shared main dish in a Chinese dim sum or family-style meal setting.
common_user_queries: How many calories in ginger chicken?
Is ginger chicken suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of Chinese ginger chicken

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for caramelised ginger chicken as a named dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy stir-fried chicken data with adjustments for caramelisation sugar and soy sauce sodium. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the core macros are well-anchored to validated stir-fried chicken data from approved sources.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy stir-fried chicken source data and is not expected to be significant in this preparation. All other -1 fields are populated with estimated values from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken data. The dish is served at Loong Dim Sum, North Canal Road, Singapore (field survey listing). Price SGD 21.00 per portion. The description 'Stir-Fried Crispy Chicken with Fresh Garlic and Caramelised Onion' from the input record is preserved as context. Nutrient values should be verified against a laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo direct entry if this dish is added to a production database. No zero-value core macros are present in this record.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch. Adapted nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy stir-fried chicken with caramelisation and soy sauce adjustments. Estimated GI from University of Sydney analogous data. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases. Documented vitamin_d_mcg = -1 sentinel value. Confirmed no zero core macros. Set GI = 45 with gi_category = 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule. All hard gates and Q1–Q8 passed on first iteration.
